title: Lighthouse among Gillette Stadium attractions to be closed during World Cup
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Gillette Stadium lighthouse and Patriots Hall of Fame will close from May 14 to July for the World Cup.
The Patriots ProShop will relocate to South Marketplace in Patriots Place during the tournament, open daily from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
Fans can participate in experiences like the FIFA Fan Festival at City Hall Plaza and stadium tailgating during the World Cup.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 13)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A home at 69 Edmund Road in Arlington sold for $2,150,000, featuring 6 bedrooms and 4 baths.
A condo at 480 W Broadway #202 in Boston sold for $750,000, built in 2022 with 2 bedrooms.
In Acton, a one-family Colonial at 96 Newtown Road, built in 2021, sold for $1,790,000.

title: North Adams man convicted, sentenced for murdering his wife in 2022
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Luis Rosado, 53, was convicted of first-degree murder for fatally stabbing his wife, Jillian Tatro, in May 2022.
A jury sentenced Rosado to life in prison without parole for the murder, according to Berkshire DA's office.
Rosado and Tatro married on January 3, 2022; her son found her body after Rosado falsely claimed she left with a friend.

title: State officials release 4 submissions to immigration agent misconduct portal launched in March
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: State officials released four submissions from the ICE misconduct portal, launched in March to report federal immigration agent activities.
A report described a lawful resident detained for 12 hours at Logan Airport without food or calls; no illegal activity found.
Complaints included ICE agents arresting a man in Cohasset and reports of armed agents near a high school in Needham.

title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Brad Stevens considers trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo to enhance the Celtics' roster post first-round playoffs exit.
Acquiring Giannis would address scoring and defensive needs but may require trading Jaylen Brown.
Concerns about Giannis's age, injury history, and contract size present risks for the Celtics.

title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Harvard faculty are voting on a proposal to limit A grades in undergraduate classes to combat grade inflation.
The plan sets a cap of 20% plus four additional As per class, impacting only A grades, not A- grades.
Over 60% of grades awarded at Harvard are As, up from 24% in 2005, prompting concerns about grading leniency.

title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Truck driver Julio Flores from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, died after a crash on I-95 South in North Attleborough.
The crash involved a 2014 Volkswagen Jetta and Flores' 2005 Freightliner M2 truck on May 5, police reported.
Flores was found with life-threatening injuries and taken to Rhode Island Hospital; investigation by State Police is ongoing.

title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Radio host Mike Felger discussed rumors of Boston Red Sox interest in trading for Angels star Mike Trout.
Felger highlighted Trout's potential impact at Fenway Park but noted injury concerns and his $37 million annual salary.
Red Sox potential trade package for Trout involves players Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello, both underperforming this season.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs joins Patriots after four years with the Packers, aiming to adapt to Josh McDaniels' offensive system.
Doubs appreciates quarterback Drake Maye's energy and looks forward to building chemistry in New England.
Doubs remains open to various roles amid rumors of Patriots' interest in trading for AJ Brown.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David Boucher, 48, charged with first degree murder, allegedly reported killing his father to North Adams police.
Victim identified as David A. Boucher, was found stabbed at 256/258 Walnut St., North Adams.
Suspect held without bail, pleaded not guilty; pretrial hearing set for June 12 in Northern Berkshire District Court.
